aesthetic.visualStyle: Brutalist concrete slick with grease and rain, steam rising from grates, neon signs bleeding color onto wet asphalt, shadows long and sharp, sepia-toned interiors contrasted by the blue-grey monotony of the storm.
aesthetic.tone: Cynical but not hopeless; moral ambiguity is the weather. Heavy, damp, weary, with moments of sharp clarity.

archetype:disgraced-detective.name: Disgraced Detective
archetype:disgraced-detective.tagline: Ex-copper hunting the truth that got him fired; uses force and intuition to break cases and faces.
archetype:disgraced-detective.description: When you use your WIT to find a clue or question a suspect, you may also spend 1 HP to force an immediate, truthful answer from anyone within earshot who has a secret they fear being exposed.

features.name: Old School Rule
features.description: When you use your WIT to find a clue or question a suspect, you may also spend 1 HP to force an immediate, truthful answer from anyone within earshot who has a secret they fear being exposed.

archetype:disgraced-detective.background: You were pushed off the force because you wouldn't take the Syndicate's money. They still owe you. Your partner died on a case you buried. You hear his footsteps in the rain sometimes.
archetype:disgraced-detective.flavor: "I don't need a badge to know when someone's lying. I just need a reason to hit them."

archetype:ink-stained-fixer.name: Ink-Stained Fixer
archetype:ink-stained-fixer.tagline: Journalist who killed his own story; uses information and influence to manipulate events and expose lies.
archetype:ink-stained-fixer.description: Once per session, you can publish a single sentence about a target (NPC or faction) that changes their public standing. If used on a major figure, it might turn allies hostile or grant you access to restricted areas for one scene.

features.name: Killer Story
features.description: Once per session, you can publish a single sentence about a target (NPC or faction) that changes their public standing. If used on a major figure, it might turn allies hostile or grant you access to restricted areas for one scene.

archetype:ink-stained-fixer.background: You have the original copy of the story that ruined you. The editor keeps it as leverage. You know who really killed the Mayor's daughter, but running the story would get you killed too.
archetype:ink-stained-fixer.flavor: "The truth isn't what happened. It's what gets printed tomorrow morning."

archetype:harbor-driver.name: Harbor Driver
archetype:harbor-driver.tagline: Wheelman who remembers every face left behind; uses speed and reflexes to escape danger and deliver goods.
archetype:harbor-driver.description: When you are in a vehicle and take damage or are pursued, you may roll AGI + CON. On a success, you shake pursuers instantly and leave any unwanted passengers or evidence behind without stopping.

features.name: Drift Away
features.description: When you are in a vehicle and take damage or are pursued, you may roll AGI + CON. On a success, you shake pursuers instantly and leave any unwanted passengers or evidence behind without stopping.

archetype:harbor-driver.background: You drove the getaway car for the Cannery District massacre. You didn't see the bodies, but you heard them. You owe a debt to the Syndicate's transport chief. He calls it in whenever he wants.
archetype:harbor-driver.flavor: "You don't look back when the sirens start. You just drive faster."

archetype:ledger-keeper.name: Ledger Keeper
archetype:ledger-keeper.tagline: Number-cruncher for the underworld; uses logic and resources to buy loyalty and sell secrets.
archetype:ledger-keeper.description: You can spend gold or a valuable item to instantly resolve a minor complication. A guard ignores you, a lock opens, or a witness forgets what they saw. The cost is always paid by someone else later.

features.name: Pay the Tab
features.description: You can spend gold or a valuable item to instantly resolve a minor complication. A guard ignores you, a lock opens, or a witness forgets what they saw. The cost is always paid by someone else later.

archetype:ledger-keeper.background: You hold the ledger that lists every bribe taken by the Aldermen. You are the insurance policy. You sold out your mentor to save yourself. Now he's dead, and you carry his silence.
archetype:ledger-keeper.flavor: "Everyone has a price. Most people just haven't been asked yet."

item:45-special.name: .45 Special
item:45-special.description: A heavy revolver with a grip worn smooth by nervous palms, the cylinder showing faint scratches from hurried reloading. The barrel is blued but dull, stained with the salt air of the docks. STR-based attack dealing 2d6 damage. On a critical hit, the target takes an additional 1d6 piercing damage and must succeed on a CON save or drop their weapon. Reload requires 2 actions.
item:45-special.flavor: "A .45 Special doesn't ask questions; it demands answers, and it always gets paid."

item:trenchcoat.name: Trenchcoat
item:trenchcoat.description: Waterlogged wool cut wide enough to swallow a man's silhouette, smelling of stale tobacco and damp fur. Deep pockets bulge with the weight of secrets, and the collar stands stiff against the neck. Grants +2 DEF against ranged attacks and environmental hazards. Allows hiding one medium item without detection. Wearer gains advantage on WIT saves against intimidation when the hood is pulled low.
item:trenchcoat.flavor: "You can carry a gun in one pocket and a lie in the other, but this coat holds both without wrinkling."

item:lucky-coin.name: Lucky Coin
item:lucky-coin.description: Brass coin rubbed smooth on one side by thumb and forefinger, stamped with the profile of the current Alderman whose face has been defaced by a knife slash across the eye. When spent, allow the user to reroll any single roll. If the reroll is lower, the coin is lost and the user suffers -1 CON permanently due to bad luck catching up.
item:lucky-coin.flavor: "Heads you pay the debt, tails you pay the blood. You've never seen it land on its edge."

consumable.effectDescription: When spent, allow the user to reroll any single roll. If the reroll is lower, the coin is lost and the user suffers -1 CON permanently due to bad luck catching up.

item:press-pass.name: Press Pass
item:press-pass.description: Laminated card with a photo faded to gray ghostliness, issued by a paper that folded three years ago but still prints. The hole-punch is ragged, held together by a twist of wire. Gain access to press events and official buildings marked "Press Only." When presenting this pass, gain advantage on WIT checks to convince low-level officials you are being watched.
item:press-pass.flavor: "This pass gets you past the copper at the door, but it won't stop him from spitting when he sees your name."

item:wristwatch.name: Wristwatch
item:wristwatch.description: Silver casing with a cracked crystal, hands frozen at 3:17 AM. It ticks silently, and the strap is stitched from leather that doesn't quite match the rest of the band. Wearer has advantage on rolls related to timing, schedules, and tracking shifts. Can determine exact local time within 1 minute even in total darkness or sensory deprivation.
item:wristwatch.flavor: "It stopped the moment the editor killed your story, but somehow it's the only honest thing left on your wrist."

item:bottle-of-bourbon.name: Bottle of Bourbon
item:bottle-of-bourbon.description: Amber liquid in a dented tin flask disguised as a glass bottle, label hand-stamped "Miriam Reserve" in peeling gold leaf. Cork is sealed with wax bearing a fingerprint. Consuming this clears one level of Stress and restores 1d4 HP. Grants advantage on CON saves for the next hour as the warmth fights the city's damp chill.
item:bottle-of-bourbon.flavor: "Bottled before the flood, aged in the vaults under City Hall. One sip tastes like money; two taste like forgiveness."

consumable.effectDescription: Consuming this clears one level of Stress and restores 1d4 HP. Grants advantage on CON saves for the next hour as the warmth fights the city's damp chill.

item:typewriter-ribbon.name: Typewriter Ribbon
item:typewriter-ribbon.description: Oil-stained ribbon spool, ink dark as dried blood, smelling of iron and ozone. The fabric strip is frayed at the edges, suggesting it was cut from something larger. When used to create a document or article, grant +2 to WIT checks for persuasion or revelation. Documents created with this ribbon cannot be forged by less skilled forgers.
item:typewriter-ribbon.flavor: "Write the truth with this ribbon and the words stick; write a lie and they'll smear the first time it rains."

item:silver-platter.name: Silver Platter
item:silver-platter.description: Engraved with the crest of the Harbor Club, tarnished black except where greedy fingers have polished the rim. A chip in the edge suggests it survived a violent removal. High-value barter item. Can be sold for significant cash or traded for favor with high-society NPCs. Carrying it openly grants advantage on CHA checks to intimidate common criminals who recognize the club crest.
item:silver-platter.flavor: "You could pawn this for a month's rent, but if you bring it back to the Mayor's dinner, they might let you keep your teeth."

item:lockpick-set.name: Lockpick Set
item:lockpick-set.description: Six tools wrapped in oilcloth, tips filed unevenly, handle carved from a bone found in the cannery gutters. Each pick has a notch cut into the tang for grip. AGI-based check to open locks. Grant +2 to pick standard locks and +1 to complex mechanisms. Allows picking a lock silently without noise checks.
item:lockpick-set.flavor: "Every lock in Port Miriam has a price; these picks just help you pay it without waking the owner."

item:molotov-cocktail.name: Molotov Cocktail
item:molotov-cocktail.description: Glass bottle filled with oily rags and distilled dock fuel, cork stuffed with newspaper soaked in alcohol. The label reads "FIRE" in crude red paint. Thrown as an attack using AGI. Deals 2d6 fire damage to targets in a 10ft radius. Creates difficult terrain and obscures vision for 1 turn. User takes 1d4 damage if thrown within 5 feet.
item:molotov-cocktail.flavor: "Not much to look at until it hits, then it burns like the whole Syndicate came to collect."

consumable.effectDescription: Thrown as an attack using AGI. Deals 2d6 fire damage to targets in a 10ft radius. Creates difficult terrain and obscures vision for 1 turn. User takes 1d4 damage if thrown within 5 feet.

item:revolver-with-one-bullet.name: Revolver with One Bullet
item:revolver-with-one-bullet.description: Snub-nosed .38, cylinder loaded with a single brass-cased round, hammer cocked. The empty chambers gleam mockingly, emphasizing the scarcity of the load. Guaranteed hit on first use dealing 1d8+STR damage. If the attack misses, the bullet is wasted and the weapon becomes a useless brick until reloaded elsewhere. Cannot be fired again until reloaded.
item:revolver-with-one-bullet.flavor: "Loaded with the last good idea anyone had about this town. Use it wisely, because there won't be a second chance."

item:rain-hat.name: Rain Hat
item:rain-hat.description: Wide-brimmed fedora, brim bent like a question mark, water sheeting off the felt instantly. The band is velvet, darkened by moisture, and holds a hidden crease for a blade. Grants +1 DEF against environmental hazards and reduces movement penalties in rain. Wearer gains advantage on Stealth checks in wet conditions by blending with shadows.
item:rain-hat.flavor: "Pull the brim low enough and nobody can see who's guilty, and pull it low enough you can't see who's coming."

item:flashlight.name: Flashlight
item:flashlight.description: Heavy metal tube, lens scratched, switch clicks twice before lighting. The beam cuts through the smog but flickers when shaken, revealing dust motes dancing like ghosts. Illuminates a 30ft cone in darkness. Reveals invisible creatures and hidden markings. Battery lasts for 6 hours of continuous use; shaking it drains power faster.
item:flashlight.flavor: "The batteries are fresh, but the light feels thin, like it's struggling to push back against the city's shadow."

item:gold-watch.name: Gold Watch
item:gold-watch.description: Gold case, enamel face, chain attached. Engraved inside: "To J.M., For Loyalty. — The Board." The chain has a clasp that requires a delicate touch to open. High-value barter item worth significantly more than its weight. Can be presented to Syndicate members to prove affiliation or demand audience. Attracts unwanted attention from collectors.
item:gold-watch.flavor: "Loyalty pays well, they said. This watch costs more than your life, which is convenient since that's what they want."

item:car-keys.name: Car Keys
item:car-keys.description: Three heavy iron keys on a leather fob, one tag reads "Dodge - Gray", another "Impound Lot 4". The key for the ignition is filed slightly shorter, requiring a jiggle to turn. Start and operate the associated vehicle immediately. Hotwire check succeeds automatically with these keys. Vehicle can be driven 10 miles before needing repair due to wear.
item:car-keys.flavor: "These keys start an engine that purrs like a rich man's pet, provided the Syndicate hasn't put a tracker in the chassis."

item:syringe.name: Syringe
item:syringe.description: Glass barrel with blue tint, needle capped, labeled "Adrenaline" in shaky script. The plunger is sticky, and a single bubble of air remains trapped near the top. Inject to restore 2d6 HP and remove one negative condition. Target must succeed on a CON save or become addicted, suffering -1 to all rolls until the addiction is treated.
item:syringe.flavor: "One shot and you feel like you could kill God; three shots and you don't care if God kills you."

consumable.effectDescription: Inject to restore 2d6 HP and remove one negative condition. Target must succeed on a CON save or become addicted, suffering -1 to all rolls until the addiction is treated.

item:briefcase.name: Briefcase
item:briefcase.description: Leather, reinforced corners, combination lock. Smells of ozone and old paper. The handle is wrapped in tape where a previous owner's grip failed. Secure storage for up to 3 medium items. Bonus +2 to CON saves against theft attempts when carrying it. Combination can be guessed with a WIT check (DC 12).
item:briefcase.flavor: "It's lighter than it looks, which means whatever's inside is either very valuable or very dangerous."

item:medal-of-honor.name: Medal of Honor
item:medal-of-honor.description: Bronze star on a frayed ribbon, back engraved with a name that doesn't match the wearer. The ribbon is stained with rust-colored spots that might be blood or might be wine. Grant advantage on CHA checks to command or inspire troops/veterans. Can be used to bluff authority or gain entry to restricted military zones.
item:medal-of-honor.flavor: "They gave him this for charging a machine gun nest; now he uses it to charge alibis."

item:umbrella-sword.name: Umbrella Sword
item:umbrella-sword.description: Black silk canopy, bamboo shaft, tip conceals a stiletto blade etched with microfilm. The handle is ivory, cool to the touch, and fits perfectly in the palm. AGI-based attack dealing 1d6 piercing damage. Can be used as a shield granting +1 DEF. Once per day, allows a surprise attack against a target who offered shelter or conversation.
item:umbrella-sword.flavor: "In this city, the polite way to slit someone's throat is to offer them shelter first."

item:business-card.name: Business Card
item:business-card.description: Thick cream stock, embossed letters, no phone number, just a street address and a symbol of a weeping eye. The card feels heavier than paper should. When given to an NPC, grants advantage on future WIT checks with that individual or their associates. If burned, reveals a hidden message in the ash after 1 hour.
item:business-card.flavor: "If you find yourself standing on the corner of 5th and Graft at midnight, burn this card and wait for the answer."

monster:dock-rat.name: Dock Rat
monster:dock-rat.description: A rat the size of a terrier, its fur matted with harbor grease and soot. Its eyes are milky white, blind but sensitive to heat, and its teeth gleam with a yellowish sheen from years of chewing through copper wiring and human bone.

attacks.name: Bite
attacks.flavor: Teeth clamp down on exposed flesh, shaking violently like a drill.

specialAbilities: Pack Tactics: If two or more Dock Rats attack the same target, they gain +2 AGI to hit that target for one round.

tactics.specialBehavior: Fight in packs. Prioritize isolated targets. Use their agility to scramble up walls and drop from above, biting and retreating before melee can be established.

monster:dock-rat.firstAction: Scatter into the shadows to flank the party, preparing to strike from multiple angles on the next round.
monster:dock-rat.lore: They nest in the hollowed-out hulls of decommissioned ships. They are attracted to the scent of blood and the metallic tang of fresh ink, often found near printing presses or hospitals.
monster:dock-rat.encounterText: The shadow under the crate shifts. It’s not just a shadow; it breathes. A wet sniffing sound precedes the snap of jaws as three more emerge from the steam vents behind you.

monster:hired-gun.name: Hired Gun
monster:hired-gun.description: A man in a cheap suit that doesn't quite fit, wearing a fedora pulled low. He carries a Thompson submachine gun with the wood stock worn smooth by nervous handling. His face is blank, devoid of personality, defined only by the scar running through his left eyebrow.

attacks.name: Tommy Gun Burst
attacks.flavor: Wood splinters fly as bullets chew through the cover, the roar deafening in the enclosed space.

attacks.name: Service Revolver
attacks.flavor: A close-range crack, sharp and final.

specialAbilities: Expendable: If reduced to 0 HP, the Hired Gun goes prone but continues to make attacks at -2 until dead, driven by adrenaline and debt.

tactics.specialBehavior: Use cover. Spray and pray with the Tommy Gun to suppress movement. If pressured, he will retreat to call for backup, valuing his life over the mission unless ordered otherwise.

monster:hired-gun.firstAction: Take cover behind a pillar and fire a suppression burst to pin the party down.
monster:hired-gun.lore: Most have debts they can't pay off with money, so they pay with violence. They don't care who they kill, as long as the check clears. Many are former cops pushed off the force for brutality.
monster:hired-gun.encounterText: The rain drums against the tin roof, masking the click of the safety being thrown off. He steps out from the doorway, the barrel of his Tommy Gun leveled at your chest. "Syndicate says you talk too much."

monster:the-weeper.name: The Weeper
monster:the-weeper.description: A spectral woman floating inches above the floorboards. She wears a wedding dress that drips not water, but black sludge. Her face is blurred, as if seen through fogged glass, and her mouth is open in a silent scream that manifests as audible sobbing.

attacks.name: Saltwater Lung
attacks.flavor: Vision blurs as liquid floods the lungs from the inside out; coughing up brine and blood.

attacks.name: Gaze of Drowning
attacks.flavor: Eyes lock onto yours, and the feeling of sinking to the bottom of the bay hits your mind.

specialAbilities: Phase Through Walls: Can move through solid objects once per encounter to reposition or ambush.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses WIT-based psychic attacks. She ignores physical defenses. She tries to isolate high-WIT characters, draining their resolve to fill her own emptiness.

monster:the-weeper.firstAction: Begin weeping loudly, forcing all nearby characters to make a CON save or suffer disadvantage on their next action due to emotional distress.
monster:the-weeper.lore: She was drowned in the harbor by a groom who wanted her insurance money. She seeks replacements because the cold is eternal, and having someone else feel the water keeps her anchored to the world of the living.
monster:the-weeper.encounterText: The temperature drops twenty degrees. The sound of crying fills the room, coming from everywhere and nowhere. Then, you see her—pale, sodden, drifting toward you, tears streaming up her cheeks instead of down.

monster:smokestack-stalker.name: Smokestack Stalker
monster:smokestack-stalker.description: A humanoid shape composed entirely of swirling gray ash and industrial smog. Two pinpricks of orange embers serve as eyes. It has no distinct features, just a shifting mass of particulate matter held together by static charge.

attacks.name: Soot Grasp
attacks.flavor: Fingers of compressed ash pierce the skin, leaving trails of burning grit.

attacks.name: Asphyxiation Cloud
attacks.flavor: Inhaling dust burns the throat; lungs feel heavy and filled with concrete.

specialAbilities: Blinding Smog: When created or when it uses a special ability, it creates a cloud of smoke. Attacks against creatures within the cloud have disadvantage.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses the environment. It blinds victims by filling rooms with smoke, then strikes from invisibility. It prefers to drag victims into tight spaces where they suffocate.

monster:smokestack-stalker.firstAction: Expands its form, filling the immediate area with dense smoke (obscuring vision) and moving to a high vantage point.
monster:smokestack-stalker.lore: Born from the exhaust of the Cannery District's factories, it feeds on oxygen and warmth. It is drawn to heated bodies and machinery, often haunting the ventilation shafts of large buildings.
monster:smokestack-stalker.encounterText: The air grows thick and tastes of sulfur. Visibility drops to zero. You hear the rustle of dry leaves, but there are no trees here. Then, a hand made of soot grabs your ankle.

monster:concrete-golem.name: Concrete Golem
monster:concrete-golem.description: A seven-foot monstrosity of gray cement and twisted rebar. Where a head should be, there is only a crude helmet fused to the skull, cracked to reveal a single, unblinking human eye. Arms end in massive, jagged fists of stone.

attacks.name: Cement Fist
attacks.flavor: A blow that feels like being hit by a car, followed by the stinging impact of broken concrete shards.

attacks.name: Rebar Lash
attacks.flavor: A rusty spike whips out from its arm, piercing armor and flesh alike.

specialAbilities: Tank: Reduces incoming damage by 2 from any source due to its thick concrete exterior.

tactics.specialBehavior: Slow but devastating. It charges directly at the nearest target, using its strength to smash through cover. It is difficult to knock back or move.

monster:concrete-golem.firstAction: Looms over the nearest character, raising a fist high, preparing to crush them.
monster:concrete-golem.lore: Once a foreman named Silas, he fell into an active foundation pour during a storm. The Syndicate covered it up, pouring more concrete over him to hide the body. He woke up inside the wall, and clawed his way out.
monster:concrete-golem.encounterText: The ground shakes rhythmically. Thud. Thud. Thud. From the construction site emerges a towering figure of wet cement and steel, dragging a length of rebar like a club. It doesn't roar; it just stares with that one trapped eye.

monster:canary.name: Canary
monster:canary.description: A small bird, bright yellow, but its feathers are slightly too large for its frame. Its beak is hooked and black, and its eyes are unnervingly intelligent. It moves with jerky, unnatural speed.

attacks.name: Toxic Peck
attacks.flavor: A quick jab to the eye or throat, followed by immediate numbness spreading through the jaw.

specialAbilities: Death Plume: When reduced to 0 HP, the Canary releases a cloud of neurotoxic powder. All adjacent creatures must make a CON save or take 1d6 poison damage and be poisoned for 1 round.

tactics.specialBehavior: Attacks from the air, targeting the face or neck. If killed, it explodes in a puff of colored gas (neurotoxin), dealing damage to anyone nearby.

monster:canary.firstAction: Perches on a high object, waiting for the perfect moment to dive-bomb the highest-value target.
monster:canary.lore: Bred in secret labs by the Syndicate's medical division. They are trained to peck at specific pressure points or ingest toxins that release upon death. One peck is enough to kill a grown man.
monster:canary.encounterText: A cheerful chirp cuts through the rain. You look up to see a bright yellow bird perched on the lamppost. It tilts its head, watching you, then dives straight for your face.

monster:rail-worm.name: Rail-Worm
monster:rail-worm.description: A segmented, armored creature resembling a centipede mixed with a worm. It is blind, with pale, fleshy antennae twitching constantly. Its mouth is a ring of rotating, chitinous teeth.

attacks.name: Chomp
attacks.flavor: Jaws clamp around a limb, grinding bone with sickening crunches.

attacks.name: Tail Whip
attacks.flavor: A sudden strike from behind, knocking the victim off balance.

specialAbilities: Vibration Sense: Cannot be surprised if the party makes noise. Advantage on attacks against moving targets.

tactics.specialBehavior: Relies on surprise and ambush. It burrows quickly, striking from below or from the sides. It prioritizes targets making noise.

monster:rail-worm.firstAction: Burrows partially underground, positioning itself to lunge at the loudest character on the next turn.
monster:rail-worm.lore: They live in the deep tunnels beneath Port Miriam, feeding on the vibrations of the trains and the bones of those who fall onto the tracks. They are blind but hear everything.
monster:rail-worm.encounterText: The subway tracks begin to vibrate. A low hum builds in the air. Before you can react, the earth beneath the platform erupts, and a massive worm bursts forth, snapping at the ankles of the nearest person.

monster:mirror-man.name: Mirror Man
monster:mirror-man.description: A humanoid figure that looks exactly like whoever is looking at it, but slightly "off"—the colors are desaturated, the movements are a half-second delayed, and the eyes are voids of black glass.

attacks.name: Mimic Strike
attacks.flavor: A familiar weapon or fist strikes from an unfamiliar angle, disorienting the victim.

attacks.name: Identity Theft
attacks.flavor: The Mirror Man whispers a secret known only to the target, causing mental shock and confusion.

specialAbilities: Reflection: Once per combat, it can teleport to any mirror or reflective surface within line of sight.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses deception and psychological warfare. It mimics the abilities of the character it is copying. It tries to confuse the party by acting like one of them.

monster:mirror-man.firstAction: Steps out of a reflective surface near the party, assuming the appearance of a PC or NPC.
monster:mirror-man.lore: It steals identities by replacing people while they sleep. It learns your mannerisms, your secrets, and your lies. It is most dangerous when it mimics a trusted ally.
monster:mirror-man.encounterText: You catch your reflection in the shop window. But you aren't standing still. Your reflection smiles, a wide, toothy grin that you aren't making. It steps out of the glass, shattering the surface.

monster:iron-lung.name: Iron Lung
monster:iron-lung.description: A hulking brute encased in a makeshift exoskeleton of iron plates and pipes. A wheezing coal furnace is strapped to its back, pumping steam and heat. Its face is hidden behind a gas mask with a cracked lens.

attacks.name: Piston Punch
attacks.flavor: A mechanical fist drives into the chest with the force of a pile driver.

attacks.name: Steam Vent
attacks.flavor: Burning vapor blasts outward, scalding skin and obscuring vision.

specialAbilities: Overheat: When reduced to half HP, the Iron Lung enters Overheat mode. It takes no damage from non-magical sources for 1 round, but deals +1d6 damage to all adjacent enemies.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses its strength to push enemies back. It emits waves of heat that can burn nearby targets. It is slow but hard to stop.

monster:iron-lung.firstAction: Stomps forward, exhaling a cloud of superheated steam from its mask.
monster:iron-lung.lore: Former dockworkers who volunteered for experimental labor-saving devices. The machines went wrong, fusing with their bodies. They are now slaves to the heat and hunger of their internal furnaces.
monster:iron-lung.encounterText: The hiss of steam announces its arrival before you see it. A massive figure clanks into view, the heat radiating from its body warping the air. It swings a hydraulic piston-arm with terrifying force.

monster:drowned-sailor.name: Drowned Sailor
monster:drowned-sailor.description: A rotting corpse dressed in tattered sailor whites, weighed down by chains wrapped around its legs and torso. Black water leaks continuously from its mouth and eyes. It hums a tuneless, discordant sea shanty.

attacks.name: Chained Grab
attacks.flavor: Cold, slimy hands wrap around the throat, pulling with surprising strength.

attacks.name: Black Water Splash
attacks.flavor: A spray of foul-smelling harbor water blinds and stings the eyes.

specialAbilities: Drag Down: If grappled, the Drowned Sailor attempts to pull the victim into adjacent water. On success, the victim is restrained and takes drowning damage each turn.

tactics.specialBehavior: Grapples victims and drags them toward water sources. They are weak individually but dangerous in groups near the water.

monster:drowned-sailor.firstAction: Shuffles toward the nearest character, humming louder, attempting to grab them.
monster:drowned-sailor.lore: Victims of the Syndicate's dumping grounds. They are drawn to the sound of water and the smell of salt. They try to pull living things into the harbor to join their eternal chorus.
monster:drowned-sailor.encounterText: A wet thud echoes from the harbor edge. You see a figure dragged by chains, stumbling out of the black water. It looks up, and you hear the faint, eerie melody of a song you almost recognize.

monster:the-auditor.name: The Auditor
monster:the-auditor.description: A tall, gaunt figure in a pristine, dust-free suit. Its face is a smooth, featureless oval of porcelain, except for a thin vertical slit where a mouth should be. It holds a leather-bound ledger and a quill that drips red ink.

attacks.name: Debt Collection
attacks.flavor: A cold sensation spreads through the body as something vital is siphoned away; limbs feel heavy and weak.

attacks.name: Contract Clause
attacks.flavor: The Auditor reads a clause aloud, and reality twists to enforce it, dealing psychic damage.

specialAbilities: Interest Rates: At the start of each round, the Auditor increases the difficulty of all skill checks by +1 for the rest of the encounter.

tactics.specialBehavior: Uses WIT to drain resources. It forces characters to make choices between losing items, stats, or time. It is very defensive, preferring to wear opponents down.

monster:the-auditor.firstAction: Opens its ledger and points a finger at the leader of the party, marking them for collection.
monster:the-auditor.lore: It is a manifestation of the Syndicate's accounting department. It collects debts not just in money, but in luck, memories, and life force. It appears when contracts are broken or favors are called in.
monster:the-auditor.encounterText: The rain stops falling around you. Silence descends. A man in a perfect suit steps out of the shadows, opening a book. He speaks, and his voice sounds like pages turning. "You have incurred a debt."

monster:fog-hound.name: Fog Hound
monster:fog-hound.description: A wolf-like creature with translucent, mist-colored fur. Its eyes are glowing blue, and its howl seems to come from inside your head rather than the air. It moves silently, blending perfectly with the fog.

attacks.name: Claw Swipe
attacks.flavor: Sharp claws tear through clothing and skin, leaving icy wounds.

attacks.name: Freezing Bite
attacks.flavor: Teeth sink into flesh, draining warmth and leaving a numbness that spreads up the limb.

specialAbilities: Fog Step: Can teleport short distances within fog or mist once per combat.

tactics.specialBehavior: Flanks and isolates targets. Uses its howl to stun or frighten. Attacks from stealth, relying on surprise.

monster:fog-hound.firstAction: Howls, causing all characters within 30 feet to make a CON save or be frightened for 1 round.
monster:fog-hound.lore: They hunt in packs, using the fog to isolate prey. They are drawn to fear and panic, feeding on the adrenaline of their victims. They are rarely seen alone.
monster:fog-hound.encounterText: The fog swirls, taking the shape of a snarling beast. A howl pierces the night, freezing your blood. You can't see it clearly, but you can feel its hot breath on your neck.

encounter:ambush-at-the-wharf.name: Ambush at the Wharf
encounter:ambush-at-the-wharf.description: Players investigating a shipment of contraband at Blackwater Wharf are pinned down by hired guns and dock rats. The rain is torrential, turning the cobblestones into slick death traps and muffling sound.
encounter:ambush-at-the-wharf.text: The neon sign above the warehouse flickers and dies, plunging the wharf into gray twilight. You hear the splash of boots in puddles behind you before you see them. A rifle crack echoes off the water. Someone is shooting from the shadows of the shipping crates.
encounter:ambush-at-the-wharf.gmGuidance: Use the rain to obscure line-of-sight for ranged attacks (disadvantage or higher DC). The environment should be hazardous; slipping on wet cobblestones or falling into the harbor is a constant risk.
outcomes: Players eliminate the ambush party but lose time and draw attention from the Syndicate's internal security.
Players escape into the drainage tunnels but leave behind crucial evidence or a wounded ally.
Players capture a live shooter who reveals the shipment contains something unexpected (not just contraband).

encounter:interrogation-in-precinct-3.name: Interrogation in Precinct 3
encounter:interrogation-in-precinct-3.description: Captain Elias Thorne has dragged a witness into the holding cells of Precinct 3. He wants the players to turn this witness against their own source, offering protection in exchange for betrayal.
encounter:interrogation-in-precinct-3.text: Captain Thorne sits on an upturned crate, smoking a cigar that smells of cheap tobacco and expensive lies. "You want the truth? The truth is in that cell. But you gotta make him sing for me first. Then I'll let you walk."
encounter:interrogation-in-precinct-3.gmGuidance: Thorne is not evil, just tired and pragmatic. He believes he is saving lives by keeping order. Use his motivation to show the cost of honesty in Port Miriam. If the players refuse, he will quietly have the witness "transferred" to a less hospitable location.
outcomes: Players convince Thorne to keep his hands off the witness, earning his grudging respect.
Players betray the witness, securing a temporary alliance with Thorne but losing moral standing.
Players force a confession out of Thorne about his own payoffs during the interrogation.

encounter:breaking-into-the-press-room.name: Breaking into the Press Room
encounter:breaking-into-the-press-room.description: The Daily Clarion building is dark, but the presses are running. Players must navigate the labyrinthine newsroom to find Editor Beatrice Bea Miller’s office without alerting the night shift guards.
encounter:breaking-into-the-press-room.text: The smell of ink is thick enough to taste. Giant metal beasts chew through paper rolls in the darkness, their rhythmic clanking masking your footsteps. Shadows stretch long across the floor, cast by the single emergency bulb humming overhead.
encounter:breaking-into-the-press-room.gmGuidance: This is a stealth and investigation challenge. Players can hide behind stacks of unsold papers or use the noise of the presses to mask movement. Look for clues about which stories were killed and why.
outcomes: Players find the editor's desk and recover the file on the missing shipment.
Players trigger a silent alarm and must fight their way out using the chaotic environment.
Players discover the editor is working with the Syndicate, framing the current story as a distraction.

choices.text: Sneak past the printing presses while they are active
choices.successOutcome: You move silently, using the roar of the machinery to cover your approach.
choices.failureOutcome: You slip on fresh ink, making a loud splat that draws the guard's attention immediately.

choices.text: Cut the power to the main floor
choices.successOutcome: The lights die. In the sudden darkness, you can move freely, though visibility is near zero.
choices.failureOutcome: The fuse box sparks and blows, triggering the backup generators which flood the room with harsh light and alarms.

choices.text: Hide inside a large stack of delivered newspapers
choices.successOutcome: You hold your breath and stay still. The guard walks right past, distracted by a phone call.
choices.failureOutcome: Your bladder gives out under pressure, or you sneeze. The guard investigates the rustling paper.

encounter:the-dead-drop.name: The Dead Drop
encounter:the-dead-drop.description: A cipher hidden in a newspaper headline points to a specific graffiti tag in the Rusty Anchor Saloon. Decoding it reveals the location of a safe deposit box key.
encounter:the-dead-drop.text: "The Mayor Drowns in Debt," reads the headline. But the letters 'D', 'A', 'E', 'N' stand out. You trace the pattern to a faded chalk symbol on the wall of the saloon—a stylized eye weeping blood.
encounter:the-dead-drop.gmGuidance: Provide the newspaper clipping and a map of the saloon. The puzzle requires cross-referencing the date of the paper with the graffiti artist's known works. Failure results in attracting the attention of the local gang that claims that territory.
outcomes: Players decode the message and find the key in the drainpipe behind the bar.
Players misinterpret the code and end up at the wrong location, fighting over a false lead.
Players solve it quickly, impressing a lurking informant who offers further assistance.

choices.text: Analyze the font style of the headline
choices.successOutcome: You recognize the typeface used only for syndicate internal memos.
choices.failureOutcome: You assume it's standard press release font, leading to a dead end.

choices.text: Search for matching graffiti in the area
choices.successOutcome: You spot the subtle variations in the spray paint that match the cipher key.
choices.failureOutcome: You find similar tags, but none contain the specific symbol required.

encounter:chase-through-the-market.name: Chase through the Market
encounter:chase-through-the-market.description: A high-speed pursuit through the crowded, slippery stalls of the market district. Players must navigate obstacles to lose a tail or catch a target.
encounter:chase-through-the-market.text: The market is a maze of awnings and bodies. Rain slicks the cobblestones, turning them into ice. Your pursuer is close—you can hear their heavy breathing over the cry of the fishmongers.
encounter:chase-through-the-market.gmGuidance: Use environmental hazards. Spilled fish, hanging meat, and narrow gaps between stalls provide opportunities for skill checks. Success allows for creative takedowns or escapes; failure means taking damage or losing momentum.
outcomes: Players lose the tail by ducking into the sewer grate.
Players catch the tail and interrogate them on the spot.
Players crash through a stall, injuring a bystander and drawing the police.

choices.text: Kick over a stack of crates to block the path
choices.successOutcome: The crates tumble, blocking the pursuer's path for several seconds.
choices.failureOutcome: The crates bounce aside, and the pursuer uses them as a stepping stone to jump over.

choices.text: Dodge through the narrow gap between stalls
choices.successOutcome: You squeeze through just in time, leaving the pursuer stuck outside.
choices.failureOutcome: Your coat catches on a nail, slowing you down and allowing them to grab your ankle.

choices.text: Throw a bucket of fish guts at them
choices.successOutcome: The sticky mess blinds the pursuer momentarily.
choices.failureOutcome: You miss, and the pursuer laughs, gaining confidence and speed.

encounter:tea-with-the-alderman.name: Tea with the Alderman
encounter:tea-with-the-alderman.description: Invited to the penthouse for tea, players realize the sugar cubes are laced with truth serum. Alderman Silas Vane is playing a game of cat and mouse.
encounter:tea-with-the-alderman.text: Alderman Vane pours the tea with a steady hand, his smile not reaching his eyes. "Sugar?" he asks, offering a silver tray. The cubes look pristine, but you notice the faint tremor in his left hand.
encounter:tea-with-the-alderman.gmGuidance: Track the effects of the serum. Each failed WIT check causes the player to reveal a secret. Vane listens intently, collecting leverage. He isn't trying to kill them, but to bind them.
outcomes: Players resist the serum and turn the tables, exposing Vane's own secrets.
Players succumb and reveal a critical weakness, which Vane notes for later use.
Players fake compliance, buying time to search the penthouse for evidence.

encounter:morgue-discovery.name: Morgue Discovery
encounter:morgue-discovery.description: Dr. Lena Kross’s morgue holds a body with unnatural markings. A missing organ is stored in a jar labeled with one of the players' names.
encounter:morgue-discovery.text: The cold air bites through your coats. On the slab lies a man whose skin is translucent, veins black as ink. Next to him, a jar of formaldehyde holds a heart. The label reads your name in handwriting you recognize.
encounter:morgue-discovery.gmGuidance: This is a horror-tinged exploration. Focus on sensory details—the smell of preservatives, the sound of dripping fluid. The jar implies a personal connection or a future fate. Players might try to identify the body or the organ donor.
outcomes: Players identify the body as a former colleague who disappeared months ago.
Players take the jar, realizing it contains a piece of themselves (metaphorically or literally).
Players find Dr. Kross watching them from the shadows, waiting for their reaction.

choices.successOutcome: You notice the incisions are too clean for a butcher, but too jagged for a surgeon.
choices.failureOutcome: You see nothing unusual beyond the obvious strangulation marks.

choices.text: Take the jar labeled with your name
choices.successOutcome: You steel yourself and pocket the jar, feeling a strange weight in your pocket.
choices.failureOutcome: Your hand shakes, and you drop the jar, shattering it and spilling the contents.

encounter:the-bank-heist-gone-wrong.name: The Bank Heist Gone Wrong
encounter:the-bank-heist-gone-wrong.description: Players stumble upon a Syndicate robbery in progress. They must choose to fight both sides, join one, or flee.
encounter:the-bank-heist-gone-wrong.text: The bank is chaos. Guards are down, and Syndicate thugs are ransacking the vault. But there’s another group here—rivals, or perhaps a rival faction of the Syndicate. Gunfire erupts as they spot each other.
encounter:the-bank-heist-gone-wrong.gmGuidance: Present the players with a tactical dilemma. Do they exploit the confusion to steal something? Do they pick a side? Combat should be fast and lethal. Emphasize the randomness of the gunfire.
outcomes: Players help the Syndicate secure the vault, earning favor.
Players loot the scene while everyone else is distracted.
Players get caught in the crossfire and suffer significant injuries.

encounter:witness-protection-bust.name: Witness Protection Bust
encounter:witness-protection-bust.description: A key witness is being moved by corrupt cops. Players must decide whether to save them or let the process run, knowing the witness will likely disappear forever.
encounter:witness-protection-bust.text: The transport van is parked outside the precinct. Two officers are loading the witness, a nervous woman named Clara Vance. She looks at you, pleading silently. The officers don't seem to care if she lives or dies.
encounter:witness-protection-bust.gmGuidance: Clara Vance is a pivotal NPC. Saving her provides a major clue but angers the police. Letting her go secures the status quo but loses a chance for justice. The tension comes from the ticking clock of the van departing.
outcomes: Players intercept the van and extract Clara, causing a scene.
Players let the van leave, but follow it discreetly.
Players bribe the officers to let them speak to Clara first.

encounter:storm-surge.name: Storm Surge
encounter:storm-surge.description: The perpetual rain intensifies into a flood, threatening to wash away evidence and drown low-lying districts. Time is running out to reach key locations.
encounter:storm-surge.text: The sky opens up. Water cascades down the streets like waterfalls. The gutters roar. You can feel the ground trembling as the tide rises rapidly beneath the city.
encounter:storm-surge.gmGuidance: This event affects all subsequent encounters. Travel times are halved, but risks are doubled. Low-lying locations may become inaccessible or dangerous. Use this to raise the stakes of a final confrontation.
outcomes: Players reach their destination before the flood cuts off the route.
Players are delayed, forcing them to take a riskier path.
The flood destroys a key piece of evidence or a location entirely.

choices.successOutcome: You outrun the surge, reaching higher ground safely.
choices.failureOutcome: You slip and fall, losing precious time and soaking wet gear.

choices.text: Secure loose objects that could become projectiles
choices.successOutcome: You prevent debris from damaging your vehicle or shelter.
choices.failureOutcome: A sign falls, narrowly missing you and blocking part of the street.

encounter:the-masquerade-ball.name: The Masquerade Ball
encounter:the-masquerade-ball.description: At a high-society gala, players must find a target among masked elites without blowing their cover. It’s a social puzzle where perception is key.
encounter:the-masquerade-ball.text: Crystal chandeliers drip light onto silk dresses and tailored suits. Everyone wears a mask, hiding their true faces. You spot your target dancing with Alderman Vane, laughing at a joke you can’t hear.
encounter:the-masquerade-ball.gmGuidance: Players must blend in. Use social skills to gather information. Watch for tells—how people move, what they say, who they avoid. The target is likely hiding something valuable.
outcomes: Players locate the target and extract a secret.
Players are discovered and must fight their way out.
Players gain entry to the inner circle, unlocking new opportunities.

encounter:abandoned-factory-raid.name: Abandoned Factory Raid
encounter:abandoned-factory-raid.description: In a derelict cannery, players face rail-worms and uncover a secret Syndicate lab. The environment is treacherous, with collapsing floors and toxic fumes.
encounter:abandoned-factory-raid.text: The factory groans around you. Shadows move in the corners. Something skitters in the pipes above. The air tastes metallic. This place has been hollowed out, repurposed for something darker.
encounter:abandoned-factory-raid.gmGuidance: Combine combat with exploration. The rail-worms are fast and deadly. The lab contains clues about the Syndicate's experiments. Use verticality—the players might need to climb or drop down levels.
outcomes: Players destroy the lab and escape with samples.
Players are trapped inside until the worms retreat or they fight their way out.
Players find the source of the Syndicate's power and expose it.

location:blackwater-wharf.name: Blackwater Wharf
location:blackwater-wharf.description: A sprawling maze of rotting timber and iron cranes where the Syndicate's wealth flows in on the tide and sinks out with the refuse. Endless rows of tar-stained crates block the wind, and the heavy scent of brine barely masks the deeper stink of curing meat and industrial runoff. The water here is black, oily, and perpetually lapping against pilings that groan under the weight of the city's greed.
location:blackwater-wharf.atmosphere: The air hangs heavy with salt and diesel. Fog rolls off the harbor in thick, yellowish waves, muffling the clatter of chain falls and the shouts of longshoremen. The rhythmic thrum of generators powers floodlights that buzz like angry hornets, casting long, swaying shadows from the gantries. Every surface is slick with moisture, whether from rain or spilled cargo.
features: Haggle with the crate-masters for contraband steel hidden inside fish barrels.
Climb the rusted gantry to spot incoming Syndicate shipments before the crane operators raise the alarm.
Sneak behind the ice-house stacks to eavesdrop on smugglers discussing a dead drop.
Barter a favor with the dock foreman for safe passage through the restricted zone.
Pick the lock on a sealed manifest crate to verify what's really being moved tonight.
Hazard: Cranes swinging blindly in the fog can crush anything in their radius without warning.
Hazard: Slick oil patches mixed with broken glass make footing treacherous; a misstep risks severe cuts or a fall into the black water.

connections.travel: A muddy slog through the back alleys of the district, passing sleeping beat cops whose boots are already soaked. The walk takes ten minutes if you mind the puddles, twenty if you're dragging something heavy.
connections.observation: You notice fresh tire tracks leading away from the precinct loading dock, marked with the Syndicate logo stamped in the grime.
connections.risk: Drunken customs inspectors may challenge you for a bribe; they're less forgiving when the rain hits their nerves.

connections.travel: Climb the rusted gangplank stairs and follow the boardwalk east. The transition is immediate: the smell of brine gives way to stale beer and sawdust, though the damp still clings to your coat.
connections.observation: Syndicate trucks idling at the edge of the saloon's lot suggest this isn't just a bar; it's a collection point for off-the-books payments.

connections.travel: Haul your gear up the steep cobblestone incline behind the warehouses. The climb burns your lungs, and the air grows thinner, smelling of boiled cabbage and coal dust instead of sea spray.
connections.observation: Laundry lines strung between the wharf sheds and the tenement towers act as tripwires for the wind, snapping taut like guitar strings.
connections.risk: Slick grease patches on the incline hide broken glass; a slip here means cuts and a fall that could break an ankle.

secrets: A crate marked 'Atlantic Cod' actually contains three missing witnesses bound and gagged, destined for the Cannery District.
The Bookkeeper maintains a private ledger hidden in a hollow bollard near Crane #4, recording every cent skimmed from the waterfront unions.

location:precinct-3-holding-cells.name: Precinct 3 Holding Cells
location:precinct-3-holding-cells.description: The brass don't want you found here. A grim concrete box beneath the main station, where the fluorescent lights flicker with a dying buzz and water drips from corroded pipes into rusted buckets. The cells are narrow, lined with peeling paint, and double as interrogation rooms where the heat lamps dry out prisoners faster than the truth dries out a witness. It smells of sweat, cheap tobacco, and institutional despair.
location:precinct-3-holding-cells.atmosphere: The hum of faulty wiring mixes with the distant wail of sirens and the muffled thud of boots on metal stairs. The air is stagnant, recycled too many times, carrying the metallic tang of fear. Shadows stretch and contract with the flickering bulbs, making the corridor feel alive with watching eyes.
features: Hazard: Overloaded circuits cause sparks to shower from the ceiling fixtures, risking fire or electrocution.
Hazard: Interrogation room heat lamps can cook a prisoner if left unchecked; a failed CON check might mean finding a victim already burned.

connections.travel: Drag your target through the back alley and across the wet pavement. The journey is quick but noisy, especially if you have to push through the swinging doors past the day shift officers.
connections.observation: A patrol car sits idling with its lights off, waiting for Captain Thorne to finish his paperwork.
connections.risk: Patrol cars may roll by; if caught moving someone without a booking form, you'll be charged with kidnapping or worse.

connections.travel: Take the service elevator up through the building's guts. The ride is slow, shaking violently, and ends in a cramped stairwell that dumps you into the newspaper's basement loading dock.
connections.observation: Captain Thorne's secretary is seen slipping a folded envelope into her purse as she exits the precinct lobby.

connections.travel: Limo ride up the hill through the rain-slicked streets. Vane's security checks papers at the gate, but if you have the right badge or enough cash, the car purrs up the drive to the penthouse garage.
connections.observation: Vane's tower blocks the sun completely for the lower districts; even here, the shadow looms large.
connections.risk: Vane's security detail is thorough; they'll search the vehicle and question anyone who looks out of place.

secrets: A loose brick in Cell 5 holds a signed confession from Mayor Sterling admitting to skimming from the relief funds.
Captain Thorne is being blackmailed by the Syndicate over a hit-and-run involving his son; he's protecting a killer who works for Vane.

location:daily-clarion-press-room.name: Daily Clarion Press Room
location:daily-clarion-press-room.description: Where the truth goes to die at 5 PM. A cavernous room dominated by clacking typewriters and towering printing presses that rumble like sleeping beasts. Cigarette smoke hangs thick enough to chew, swirling around the heads of exhausted writers and editors wielding red pens like scalpels. The air tastes of ink and desperation, and every headline feels like a compromise.
location:daily-clarion-press-room.atmosphere: Rhythmic clacking provides a heartbeat to the room, punctuated by the sharp tear of paper and the hiss of steam valves. Fluorescent tubes buzz overhead, casting a sickly green pallor over faces pale from lack of sleep. The constant drip of a leaky pipe in the corner marks time more reliably than any clock.
features: Hazard: Falling printing plates or heavy rollers can crush anyone standing in the wrong spot during a malfunction.
Hazard: Smoke inhalation is severe; prolonged exposure requires a CON save or suffer penalties to perception and speech.
Hazard: The Editor's wrath is swift; failing a social roll can cost you your job or your reputation overnight.

connections.travel: Run copy down to the police desk via the service chute. The slide is fast but rough on the clothes, depositing you in a pile of discarded proof sheets near the holding cell entrance.
connections.observation: Captain Thorne is arguing with a reporter about the release of a suspect's name.
connections.risk: Running into Thorne during a sensitive exchange could lead to a confrontation or a lost scoop.

connections.travel: Meet sources in the back booth after closing. The walk is short, but the mood shifts from professional urgency to conspiratorial whispering as you cross the threshold.
connections.observation: Slick Mickey is nursing a drink at the bar, watching the door with a knowing smirk.

connections.travel: Deliver the embargo story to Vane's press attaché. You must navigate the penthouse lobby, presenting credentials to a silent guard who checks them with suspicious precision.
connections.observation: Vane's tower gleams despite the gloom, a monument to wealth that seems untouched by the city's rot.
connections.risk: Vane's people don't like unscheduled deliveries; being caught without proper authorization could result in expulsion or worse.

secrets: The Editor killed a story about the Tenements eviction notices because Vane owns his daughter's medical debts.
Clara Vance uses a dead drop in the mailroom to leak info to the resistance; the drop is inside a hollowed-out copy of the city phone book.

location:the-gilded-cage-penthouse.name: The Gilded Cage Penthouse
location:the-gilded-cage-penthouse.description: Alderman Vane's perch above the rain. Velvet drapes swallow sound, crystal glasses catch the dim light, and walls seem to eat whispers. It's a sanctuary of opulence where the city's decay is kept at bay by thick glass and expensive insulation. Here, decisions are made that ruin lives, served on porcelain with a smile that never reaches the eyes.
location:the-gilded-cage-penthouse.atmosphere: Muffled jazz plays from a hidden gramophone, competing with the low thrum of the city below. The air is cool, conditioned, and scented with expensive perfume and beeswax. Crystal chandeliers cast prismatic shards of light across Persian rugs, while outside, the perpetual storm rages, indifferent to the gold leaf framing the windows.
features: Hazard: Glass shards from dropped chandeliers can cause severe cuts; a failed AGI save results in bleeding out quickly.
Hazard: Vane's personal bodyguard carries a silencer; catching him off-guard is vital, or he'll put a bullet in your brain before you blink.
Hazard: Social suicide is a real threat; being caught trespassing without status could end your career or life in this city.

connections.travel: Private elevator descends to the helipad and transfers you to a waiting launch. The ride is smooth, but the view of the wharf from above reveals the true scale of the Syndicate's operation.
connections.observation: Syndicate money bags are being loaded onto crates, labeled with false shipping documents.

connections.travel: Call Captain Thorne to report a problem; his voice crackles over the secure line, tight with suppressed anger. If you visit, take the armored car through the rear entrance.
connections.observation: Thorne's face is drawn on the monitor; he looks like a man holding onto a fraying rope.
connections.risk: Thorne may not welcome a visit; he's prone to violence when his leverage is threatened.

connections.travel: Host the Editor for a quiet word. The car ride up is tense; the Editor knows he's walking into the lion's den, clutching his briefcase like a shield.
connections.observation: Vane's staff watches the Editor with predatory interest, noting every nervous gesture.

secrets: Vane isn't just corrupt; he's hiding a subtle supernatural artifact in the vault that amplifies his influence, keeping the rain endless and the populace docile.
He has a second family in the Tenements he pays to keep quiet; discovering this could shatter his public image and political power.

location:rusty-anchor-saloon.name: Rusty Anchor Saloon
location:rusty-anchor-saloon.description: Honest drinks, dishonest talk. Sawdust floors soak up decades of spilled gin, and a jukebox plays static-laced ballads that sound like ghosts weeping. The bartender knows everyone's price, and the booths are deep enough to hide a conspiracy or a corpse. It's a place where deals are struck in shadows and loyalty is tested over cheap whiskey.
location:rusty-anchor-saloon.atmosphere: Low light pools around tables, creating islands of privacy in a sea of murk. The smell of gin, sawdust, and frying food mixes with the damp wool of wet coats drying by the stove. Laughter rings out, sometimes genuine, often forced, mingling with the clink of glass and the shuffle of cards.
features: Hazard: Bar fights break out frequently; a stray bullet or flying bottle can injure bystanders instantly.
Hazard: The jukebox plays a specific song that signals a raid; ignoring the cue can leave you trapped with the cops.
Hazard: Spilled alcohol near open flames poses a fire risk; a careless move could ignite the whole room.

connections.travel: Walk along the boardwalk back to the docks. The rain hammers down, and drunks stumble out of the side doors, blocking your path until you pay or fight.
connections.observation: Syndicate enforcers are collecting 'protection' from a fishmonger, kicking over his crates.
connections.risk: Drunks throwing bottles can start a brawl; intervene and you might get punched, ignore it and you might miss a clue.

connections.travel: Meet the gossip columnist for lunch. The walk is short, but the columnists watch you like hawks; one wrong move and you're the subject of tomorrow's smear.
connections.observation: Typewriter keys are still warm inside the press room, suggesting someone worked late on a big story.

connections.travel: Stumble home up the hill through the rain. The descent is easy, but the ascent is brutal, and gang kids look for loose change in pockets.
connections.observation: Street preachers shout about the end times, pointing fingers at the penthouse towers piercing the clouds.
connections.risk: Gang kids may mug you; they're desperate and armed with rusty knives.

secrets: Beatrice Bea Miller hides a wounded witness in the cellar, treating his injuries with herbs and hope.
The saloon is a neutral ground protected by an old blood debt to the Syndicate; killing someone here without permission invites vengeance from Vane himself.

location:saint-judes-tenements.name: Saint Jude's Tenements
location:saint-judes-tenements.description: Where the poor drown in debt and disease. Leaning brick towers rise like broken teeth against the gray sky, connected by laundry lines strung between buildings like tripwires. The constant drip of leaking roofs echoes through the halls, and the air is thick with the smell of boiled cabbage, mold, and sickness. It's a vertical slum where every inch is fought for, and survival is a daily victory.
location:saint-judes-tenements.atmosphere: Claustrophobic verticality defines the space; stairwells spiral up into darkness, and windows look directly into neighbors' lives. The sound of coughing rises from every floor, mingling with the wails of children and the shouting of landlords. Light filters through stained glass from a crooked chapel nearby, casting colored patches on the wet bricks.
features: Hazard: Structural collapse is common; weakened floors can give way under weight, sending occupants plummeting.
Hazard: Tuberculosis outbreaks spread quickly; failing a WIT check might mean contracting the disease yourself.
Hazard: Landlord enforcers evict families ruthlessly; resisting can lead to property damage or injury.

connections.travel: Haul goods down the hill to sell at the market. The descent is slippery, and falling debris from the upper floors can crush anything below.
connections.observation: Syndicate trucks are unloading cheap, spoiled food into the tenement courtyards, inflating prices.
connections.risk: Falling debris or loose bricks can strike without warning; watch your step and head.

connections.travel: Descent to the street level via the main staircase. The journey is loud and crowded, filled with the sounds of daily struggle.
connections.observation: Street preachers gather at the base of the stairs, handing out pamphlets and warnings.
connections.risk: Pickpockets operate in the crowd; keep your wallet close or lose it to a nimble finger.

connections.travel: Look up at the shining spire from the slums. The contrast is stark; Vane's tower blocks the sun for the poor, casting a permanent shadow over their lives.
connections.observation: Children point at the penthouse, telling stories of the monster who lives in the clouds.

secrets: Father Thomas O'Connell runs a secret school for orphans using Vane's slush fund; discovering this could turn the priest into a powerful ally or a target.
A tunnel connects the basement directly to the Cannery District sewers, used by smugglers to move goods unseen.

npc:alderman-silas-vane.name: Alderman Silas Vane
npc:alderman-silas-vane.description: Tall and gaunt, wearing a suit that costs more than most men earn in a year. His smile is too wide, stretching the skin around his eyes until they disappear into crinkles of false warmth.
npc:alderman-silas-vane.personality: Speaks slowly, savoring each word like a fine wine he bought with stolen money. He treats people like inventory—assessing their value and discarding them when depreciated.

motivation.want: To consolidate total control over Port Miriam's infrastructure, turning the city into a self-sustaining machine of profit.
motivation.fear: Being seen as merely a politician rather than the inevitable force of nature the Syndicate has become.
motivation.lie: "I am doing this for the city's stability. Chaos is expensive; order is profitable."

dialogueHints: "Honesty is a luxury item, Detective. Can you afford it?"
"We don't break the law. We just... adjust the terms of service."

relationships.npc:mayor-eleanor-sterling: Vane owns Sterling’s office; she signs the checks, he dictates the amounts.
relationships.npc:the-bookkeeper: The Bookkeeper reports directly to Vane; Vane trusts the numbers more than the man.
relationships.npc:slick-mickey-omalley: Mickey is Vane’s errand boy; useful for dirty work but easily replaced.
relationships.npc:clara-vance: Vane killed Vance’s biggest story five years ago; she hates him, he finds her amusingly persistent.

secrets: Vane isn’t just corrupt; he’s addicted to the rain. He believes the storm is cleansing the city, and he is its high priest.

npc:captain-elias-thorne.name: Captain Elias Thorne
npc:captain-elias-thorne.description: Broad-shouldered but slumped, with a face carved from granite and regret. One eye is milky white from an old shiv wound; the other is sharp and tired.
npc:captain-elias-thorne.personality: Gruff, quiet, and deeply cynical about the system he swore to uphold. He drinks cheap whiskey to drown out the sound of sirens he knows are lying.

motivation.want: To find the one case that proves the corruption goes deeper than he thought, even if it destroys him.
motivation.fear: That he was wrong to stay honest, and that his integrity cost his family everything.
motivation.lie: "If I keep my head down and do my job, the truth will eventually surface on its own."

dialogueHints: "The badge doesn't mean much anymore. It’s just tin."*
"Don't look at me like I’m saving you. I’m just looking for my own ghosts."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane pushed Thorne off the force for refusing to bury a body. Thorne blames himself for not fighting harder.
relationships.npc:ghost-rourke: Rourke drove the getaway car for the hit team that framed Thorne; Thorne knows it was Rourke but lacks proof.
relationships.npc:beatrice-bea-miller: Bea feeds Thorne tips when the official records lie. He trusts her ears more than the precinct logs.

secrets: Thorne still has the original evidence file hidden in a locker at Blackwater Wharf, but he’s afraid to open it because it implicates someone he loved.

npc:slick-mickey-omalley.name: "Slick" Mickey O'Malley
npc:slick-mickey-omalley.description: Lean and wiry, with a nervous twitch in his left hand. Always wears a fedora pulled low, hiding a scar that runs from ear to jaw.
npc:slick-mickey-omalley.personality: Fast-talking, charming, and opportunistic. He smiles with his teeth but never his eyes. He can sell ice to a drowning man if the price is right.

motivation.want: To climb out of the gutter and buy a ticket out of Port Miriam permanently.
motivation.fear: Being caught between two powers and crushed by both.
motivation.lie: "Loyalty is just a contract with no written expiration date."

dialogueHints: "I don't care who did it. I care who pays me to find out."
"You want the truth? The truth costs extra."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane uses Mickey for jobs that don’t require a name on the payroll.
relationships.npc:dr-lena-kross: Mickey brought Kross a body that vanished without autopsy notes; he suspects she knows more than she says.
relationships.npc:father-thomas-oconnell: O'Connell lent Mickey money to start his fixing business; Mickey owes favors, not cash.

secrets: Mickey is secretly being blackmailed by the Bookkeeper over a gambling debt that spiraled out of control.

npc:clara-vance.name: Clara Vance
npc:clara-vance.description: Sharp features, severe bob cut, always dressed in impeccable black wool despite the damp. She carries a silver fountain pen like a weapon.
npc:clara-vance.personality: Calculating, cold, and fiercely intelligent. She speaks in headlines and subtext. She kills stories not just for power, but to protect secrets she deems dangerous.

motivation.want: To uncover the "Miriam Protocol," a secret agreement between the Aldermen and the federal government.
motivation.fear: That the truth she’s chasing will destroy the paper that defines her life.
motivation.lie: "Control the narrative, and you control reality."

dialogueHints: "Your story is good. But is it profitable?"
"I didn't kill your lead. I just made sure nobody wanted to read it."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane killed her first major expose; she has been hunting him ever since.
relationships.npc:mayor-eleanor-sterling: Sterling funds the Clarion through shell companies; Vance knows this but hasn’t printed it yet.
relationships.npc:ghost-rourke: Rourke once delivered a package to her desk containing a photo of her brother in the Syndicate’s custody.

secrets: Vance is losing her sight. She hides it behind dark glasses and relies on memory, making her a brilliant but fragile investigator.

npc:dr-lena-kross.name: Dr. Lena Kross
npc:dr-lena-kross.description: Pale, thin, with steady hands and eyes that seem to look through you. She smells of formaldehyde and lavender.
npc:dr-lena-kross.personality: Clinical, precise, and unnervingly calm. She views death as a puzzle to be solved, not an event to be mourned. She collects strange artifacts in her freezer.

motivation.want: To identify the source of the "Gray Rot" affecting the lower district bodies.
motivation.fear: That the artifact she found in a victim’s chest is alive.
motivation.lie: "Science explains everything, if you have enough time and samples."

dialogueHints: "This wound wasn't made by a knife. It was made by something hungry."
"Keep the body cool. And don't ask where I got the specimen jar."

relationships.npc:captain-elias-thorne: Thorne trusts her autopsies implicitly; she provides him with the only objective truths in the precinct.
relationships.npc:the-bookkeeper: The Bookkeeper pays her double to suppress certain names on death certificates.

secrets: Kross discovered that the "Gray Rot" is spreading through the water supply, originating from the Cannery District docks.

npc:ghost-rourke.name: "Ghost" Rourke
npc:ghost-rourke.description: Tall, lean, with grease-stained knuckles and a perpetual stubble. Wears a leather jacket that looks like it survived a war.
npc:ghost-rourke.personality: Quiet, observant, and professional. He rarely speaks unless necessary. He remembers every favor owed and every slight taken.

motivation.want: To find the woman who left him at the scene of the '45 fire, the one thing he couldn't drive away from.
motivation.fear: Being pinned down, trapped in one place where he can't escape.
motivation.lie: "People are cargo. They go where you tell them to go."

dialogueHints: "I don't need cash. I need a name. Or a favor."
"You get in the car, you shut up, and you don't look back."

relationships.npc:captain-elias-thorne: Thorne recognizes Rourke’s driving style from the night his partner died.
relationships.npc:clara-vance: Vance paid Rourke to deliver the photo of her brother; he holds the leverage.
relationships.npc:slick-mickey-omalley: Mickey owes Rourke a significant favor for a job gone wrong in '46.

secrets: Rourke’s car is modified with a hidden compartment containing the last journal of the missing union leader.

npc:beatrice-bea-miller.name: Beatrice "Bea" Miller
npc:beatrice-bea-miller.description: Small, round, with large ears and wire-rimmed glasses. She sits surrounded by a tangle of wires and switchboards.
npc:beatrice-bea-miller.personality: Eavesdropping, gossipy, but surprisingly astute. She hears everything and judges quietly. She weaves gossip into intelligence.

motivation.want: To prove that her hearing aids aren't failing her before the Syndicate replaces her.

dialogueHints: "Did you hear? The Mayor’s husband wasn’t sick. He was poisoned."
"I can patch you through, but you’ll owe me a candy bar."

relationships.npc:captain-elias-thorne: Thorne visits her often for off-the-record tips; she keeps his name clean in the logs.
relationships.npc:clara-vance: Vance listens to Bea’s calls to monitor public sentiment and press leaks.
relationships.npc:father-thomas-oconnell: O’Connell calls her from the church rectory to check on parishioners’ debts.

secrets: Bea recorded a conversation between Vane and the Bookkeeper discussing the "Harbor Cleanup" scheduled for next week.

npc:father-thomas-oconnell.name: Father Thomas O'Connell
npc:father-thomas-oconnell.description: Heavy-set, with a booming voice and a kind face that masks a calculating mind. He wears a simple cassock but gold rings on every finger.
npc:father-thomas-oconnell.personality: Charismatic and paternal, but with a hard edge. He offers salvation and usury in equal measure. He believes he is helping the poor by keeping them dependent.

motivation.want: To expand the Church’s influence into the political sphere, using debt as the leash.
motivation.fear: That the Syndicate will discard the Church once its utility is exhausted.
motivation.lie: "Debt is a sin, but ownership is a blessing."

dialogueHints: "God forgives, Thomas. But the ledger does not."
"Come to confession. Or come to pay. Both doors are open."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane needs the Church’s votes; O’Connell needs Vane’s protection for his loan sharks.
relationships.npc:slick-mickey-omalley: O’Connell lent Mickey his start-up money; Mickey is deep in debt.
relationships.npc:beatrice-bea-miller: O’Connell uses Bea to track which families are falling behind on payments.

secrets: O’Connell is secretly funding the opposition candidate in the next election, trying to create a wedge against Vane.

npc:mayor-eleanor-sterling.name: Mayor Eleanor Sterling
npc:mayor-eleanor-sterling.description: Polished porcelain beauty, with perfect hair and a smile that never reaches her eyes. She moves with the grace of a dancer.
npc:mayor-eleanor-sterling.personality: Charming, poised, and utterly ruthless. She plays the role of the benevolent leader perfectly, but she is Vane’s puppet and knows it.

motivation.want: To maintain her status and wealth, even if it means letting the city rot beneath her heels.
motivation.fear: Being exposed as a fraud, unworthy of her position.
motivation.lie: "I am the city. Without me, there is only chaos."

dialogueHints: "Politics is a dance, darling. And I always lead."
"Vane handles the dirty work. I handle the pretty faces."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane is her master; she performs publicly, he rules privately.
relationships.npc:clara-vance: Sterling funds the Clarion; Vance knows this and uses it to manipulate her stories.
relationships.npc:father-thomas-oconnell: O’Connell supports her publicly but plots against her privately.

secrets: Sterling is dying of a wasting disease. She doesn’t know how long she has left, which makes her desperate to secure her legacy.

npc:the-bookkeeper.name: The Bookkeeper
npc:the-bookkeeper.description: Tall and thin, wearing a plain cardboard mask with two eye holes. Dressed in a gray suit that blends into the fog. Speaks in a monotone.
npc:the-bookkeeper.personality: Emotionless, precise, and terrifyingly efficient. He sees people as numbers. He knows exactly how much your soul is worth.

motivation.want: To balance the Ledger of Port Miriam, ensuring the Syndicate’s profit matches its moral decay.
motivation.lie: "Everything has a price. If you don't know yours, I'll set it."

dialogueHints: "Your loyalty is valued at three months' salary. Payable upon death."
"The numbers don't lie. People do."

relationships.npc:alderman-silas-vane: Vane trusts the Bookkeeper’s numbers more than his own intuition.
relationships.npc:dr-lena-kross: Pays Kross to suppress names on death certificates, altering the city’s mortality data.
relationships.npc:slick-mickey-omalley: Blackmails Mickey over a gambling debt; Mickey is terrified of him.

secrets: The Bookkeeper is not a person, but a title passed down through generations of the same family, all wearing the mask.

faction:the-aldermens-syndicate.name: The Aldermen's Syndicate
faction:the-aldermens-syndicate.description: A cabal of industrialists and politicians who treat the city as their personal ledger; wealth, power, and ruthlessness are their weapons. They believe order is purchased, not earned.
goals: Maintain the monopoly on harbor logistics to control all imports and exports.
Neutralize independent journalism that threatens their public image.
Expand territory into the Saint Jude's Tenements for a new shipping terminal.
resources: Control over the Blackwater Wharf cranes and storage facilities.
Bribed judges and half the precinct police force.
Influence over Mayor Sterling’s budget approvals.

faction:the-blue-wall-brotherhood.reason: The Brotherhood enforces the Syndicate's will in the streets; the Syndicate pays the officers' pensions.

faction:the-free-press-coalition.reason: The Coalition exposes the graft the Syndicate hides in plain sight.

hooks: Investigate why three rival dock bosses vanished simultaneously from the holding cells at Precinct 3.
Discover that the "rain" in Port Miriam is actually runoff from a chemical processing plant owned by a shell company.
Follow a trail of missing funds through The Bookkeeper's ledgers to find a secret vault.
secrets: The Mayor isn't just bribed; she owns the debt of the entire City Council.
The Syndicate is bankrupting itself to fund a supernatural entity buried under the wharf foundations.

faction:the-free-press-coalition.name: The Free Press Coalition
faction:the-free-press-coalition.description: Journalists and printers fighting to keep the truth alive; scattered, underfunded, but willing to risk everything for a headline. They operate out of the Daily Clarion, a building held together by spite and ink.
goals: Publish the "Ledger of Lies," a definitive exposé of the Syndicate's corruption.
Secure funding to buy back their printing presses from loan sharks.
Protect their sources, even when it costs lives.
resources: Access to the city's most cynical reporters and photographers.
The Daily Clarion press room, which serves as a neutral ground for whispers.
A network of informants among the dockworkers and street kids.

faction:the-aldermens-syndicate.reason: The Syndicate kills stories before they print and intimidates advertisers.

faction:the-blue-wall-brotherhood.reason: Cops hate being watched; the Coalition has cameras everywhere.

hooks: Clara Vance needs a driver to get a film canister out of the city before the Syndicate burns the office.
Beatrice Miller found a pattern in the obituaries that predicts Syndicate hits.
secrets: Clara Vance was once paid off by the Syndicate and is hiding a scandal that could destroy her credibility.
Ghosts Rourke isn't dead; he's been working as a double agent for the Brotherhood for five years.

faction:the-blue-wall-brotherhood.name: The Blue Wall Brotherhood
