faction:the-blue-wall-brotherhood.description: Union of cops, mostly corrupt but with pockets of loyalty; they protect their own, demand respect, and answer to the highest bidder. To them, the law is what they say it is, inside the precinct walls.
goals: Maintain the "Blue Wall" — no cop testifies against another cop.
Secure the upcoming union election to ensure a pro-Syndicate captain wins.
Crack down on vice in the Tenements to justify increased overtime pay.
resources: Physical control of Precinct 3 and its holding cells.
Numbers and badges that command fear in the streets.
Private connections to the docks for smuggling protection money.

faction:the-aldermens-syndicate.reason: The Syndicate provides the money; the Brotherhood provides the muscle.

faction:the-free-press-coalition.reason: The Coalition prints the stories that make the cops look bad.

hooks: Captain Thorne hires the party to retrieve a stolen evidence bag containing a key witness's location.
Slick Mickey O'Malley needs a distraction while he plants contraband in a rival gang's car.
Father O'Connell asks the party to investigate a body found in the river that shouldn't be there.
secrets: Captain Thorne is dying of lung rot and is selling his pension to pay for treatment.
Slick Mickey is the one killing the honest cops who get too close to the truth.

situation:the-missing-ledger.name: The Missing Ledger
situation:the-missing-ledger.description: The Syndicate’s master accounting book, detailing every bribe, shakedown, and illicit payment from the Mayor down to the beat cops, has vanished from its safe. The city is a powder keg of paranoia; everyone thinks someone else knows.

risks: The Syndicate’s master accounting book, detailing every bribe, shakedown, and illicit payment from the Mayor down to the beat cops, has vanished from its safe. The city is a powder keg of paranoia; everyone thinks someone else knows.

actors.agenda: Determine if his name is in the book and who took it.
actors.leverage: He controls the police precincts and the dock unions.

defaultAction.now: Orders Captain Thorne to raid three low-level fixers’ apartments looking for the book.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Burns the warehouse of a minor rival to send a message that he will destroy anyone with loose lips.

actors.agenda: Survive the purge by keeping the ledger out of Vane’s hands but visible enough to be useful.
actors.leverage: He knows where the book is, or at least where it was last seen.

defaultAction.now: Hiding in the back room of the Daily Clarion, waiting for a buyer who won’t ask questions.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Sells the ledger to the highest bidder, regardless of who they are.

clock.name: The Leak Spreads
clock.doom: Vane executes three innocent people to show he doesn’t care who gets hurt, and the city descends into open warlordism.
clock.pauseCondition: Players recover the ledger or expose Vane’s specific involvement in the murders.

stages.name: Whispers
stages.description: Rumors start circulating in the saloons about missing money.

trigger.eventDescription: A body is found with a single page of the ledger sewn into their coat.

consequences.narrative: Cops are nervous; reporters are sniffing around the Gilded Cage.
The Police Precinct 3 holding cells are full. Trust is gone.

trigger.eventDescription: The Bookkeeper is found dead (or alive but mute).

consequences.narrative: The docks stop working. Smoke fills the harbor.

outgoingLeads.information: Mayor Sterling paid $50,000 to silence a reporter regarding her illegitimate child.

discovery.description: A torn page found in the gutter outside the Mayor's office.

outgoingLeads.information: Alderamn Vane receives weekly payments from the Cannery District owner.

discovery.description: A list of payees in the Bookkeeper's possession.

victory.description: The The Missing Ledger is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Missing Ledger ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The Syndicate’s master accounting book, detailing every bribe, shakedown, and illicit payment from the Mayor down to the beat cops, has vanished from its safe. The city is a powder keg of paranoia; everyone thinks someone else knows.

situation:the-strike-at-the-docks.name: The Strike at the Docks
situation:the-strike-at-the-docks.description: Harbor workers have stopped unloading cargo, demanding fair wages. The Syndicate has sent in 'enforcers'—undocumented thugs—to break the strike by any means necessary. Tensions are high, and violence is imminent.

risks: Harbor workers have stopped unloading cargo, demanding fair wages. The Syndicate has sent in 'enforcers'—undocumented thugs—to break the strike by any means necessary. Tensions are high, and violence is imminent.

actors.agenda: Keep the peace long enough to get a better deal, then betray the workers if needed.
actors.leverage: He controls the union votes and the enforcers.

defaultAction.now: Negotiating with worker leaders while secretly signaling enforcers to wait.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Orders the enforcers to shoot on sight if the strikers don't disperse by dawn.

actors.agenda: Treat the wounded, regardless of side, and document the brutality.
actors.leverage: She controls the medical supplies and the truth of who started the shooting.

defaultAction.now: Setting up a triage tent near the wharf entrance.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Leaves the tenements to move closer to the front lines, risking being caught in the crossfire.

clock.name: The Breaking Point
clock.doom: Full-scale riot. The Navy is called in. The district is militarized.
clock.pauseCondition: Players mediate a truce or eliminate the lead enforcer.

stages.name: Tension
stages.description: Workers block the gates; enforcers gather outside.

trigger.eventDescription: A bottle is thrown, or a gun is fired.

consequences.narrative: Injuries mount. Dr. Kross is overwhelmed.

stages.name: War
stages.description: The enforcers advance with batons and guns.

consequences.narrative: The wharf becomes a kill zone.

outgoingLeads.information: The enforcers are not local police; they are mercenaries hired specifically for this job.

discovery.description: Checking the IDs of a fallen enforcer reveals no precinct number.

outgoingLeads.information: Dr. Kross has X-rays showing the enforcers were using live rounds, not blanks.

discovery.description: Kross offers the X-rays to anyone who can protect her lab.

victory.description: The The Strike at the Docks is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Strike at the Docks ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Harbor workers have stopped unloading cargo, demanding fair wages. The Syndicate has sent in 'enforcers'—undocumented thugs—to break the strike by any means necessary. Tensions are high, and violence is imminent.

situation:the-mayors-secret-child.name: The Mayor's Secret Child
situation:the-mayors-secret-child.description: Mayor Sterling has a illegitimate child hidden in the tenements; discovery could topple her administration and shake the Syndicate.

risks: Mayor Sterling has a illegitimate child hidden in the tenements; discovery could topple her administration and shake the Syndicate.

actors.agenda: Protect her child at all costs, even if it means sacrificing her career.
actors.leverage: She controls the city council and the press.

defaultAction.now: Using her influence to keep the tenement landlord quiet.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Orders the Police to "check" the tenement for health violations as a pretext to search.

actors.agenda: Keep the child safe within the church walls.
actors.leverage: He commands the respect of the tenement residents.

defaultAction.now: Feeding the child and hiding them in the church basement.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Offers the child sanctuary in the cathedral, defying the Mayor directly.

clock.name: The Scandal Breaks
clock.doom: The Mayor resigns in disgrace, leaving a power vacuum that the Syndicate fills immediately.
clock.pauseCondition: Players secure the child’s identity or convince the Mayor to go public on her terms.

stages.name: Gossip
stages.description: Whispers in the saloons about the Mayor’s “nephew.”

trigger.eventDescription: A photo of the Mayor visiting the tenement is spotted.

consequences.narrative: The Mayor is distracted and making errors in public.

stages.name: Explosion
stages.description: The story hits the front page.

trigger.eventDescription: A reporter publishes the story with a photo of the child.

consequences.narrative: Public outrage. The Mayor’s authority crumbles.

outgoingLeads.information: The child’s name is Maya, and she is sick with the plague.

discovery.description: Father Thomas mentions the girl’s cough when speaking to priests.

outgoingLeads.information: The Mayor paid off the midwife who delivered the child.

discovery.description: A receipt in the Mayor’s private files.

victory.description: The The Mayor's Secret Child is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Mayor's Secret Child ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Mayor Sterling has a illegitimate child hidden in the tenements; discovery could topple her administration and shake the Syndicate.

situation:the-plague-in-the-tenements.name: The Plague in the Tenements
situation:the-plague-in-the-tenements.description: A mysterious illness is spreading in Saint Jude's; the Syndicate is quarantining the district to contain the outbreak, trapping thousands inside.

risks: A mysterious illness is spreading in Saint Jude's; the Syndicate is quarantining the district to contain the outbreak, trapping thousands inside.

actors.agenda: Find the source of the plague and treat the patients.
actors.leverage: She has the medical knowledge and the only effective treatment.

defaultAction.now: Working in the makeshift clinic, trying to ration medicine.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Leaves the clinic to investigate the water supply, risking infection.

actors.agenda: Smuggle goods in and out of the quarantine zone for profit.
actors.leverage: He knows the alleys and the guards’ bribes.

defaultAction.now: Running contraband cigarettes and alcohol into the tenements.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Is caught by the Syndicate and beaten, forcing him to reveal who he’s working for.

clock.name: The Walls Breach
clock.doom: The quarantine walls fall. The plague escapes into the rest of the city.
clock.pauseCondition: Players reinforce the walls or find a way to safely evacuate the sick.

stages.name: Containment
stages.description: The plague is contained within the tenements.

stages.name: Strain
stages.description: Guards start getting sick; morale drops.

trigger.eventDescription: A guard is found dead at the gate.

consequences.narrative: Corpses are piling up outside the walls.

stages.name: Breach
stages.description: The walls are breached by panicked civilians or disease.

outgoingLeads.information: The plague is caused by contaminated water from the Cannery District.

discovery.description: Dr. Kross tests the water and finds industrial waste.

outgoingLeads.information: Dr. Kross has a potential cure but needs a rare ingredient from the Blackwater Wharf.

discovery.description: Kross asks for help retrieving the ingredient.

victory.description: The The Plague in the Tenements is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Plague in the Tenements ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: A mysterious illness is spreading in Saint Jude's; the Syndicate is quarantining the district to contain the outbreak, trapping thousands inside.

situation:the-election-rigging.name: The Election Rigging
situation:the-election-rigging.description: The upcoming mayoral election is being rigged; players can expose the fraud or play the game and profit from the manipulation.

risks: The upcoming mayoral election is being rigged; players can expose the fraud or play the game and profit from the manipulation.

actors.agenda: Get the story that makes her career, or bury it if it’s too dangerous.
actors.leverage: She controls the narrative in the city.

defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Buries the big story because her editor was bought off.

actors.agenda: Ensure the election goes smoothly for the Syndicate.
actors.leverage: He controls the police force and the ballot boxes.

defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Forces the recount to favor the Syndicate candidate.

clock.name: The Vote Count
clock.doom: The Syndicate candidate wins by a landslide, despite losing the popular vote.
clock.pauseCondition: Players expose the fraud or secure the votes.

stages.name: Counting
stages.description: The votes are counted behind closed doors.

outgoingLeads.information: The ballot boxes are being swapped before counting.

discovery.description: Bea Miller sees the boxes being loaded onto different trucks.
Bea mentions meeting a "tall man in a grey suit."

victory.description: The The Election Rigging is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Election Rigging ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: The upcoming mayoral election is being rigged; players can expose the fraud or play the game and profit from the manipulation.

situation:the-syndicate-purge.name: The Syndicate Purge
situation:the-syndicate-purge.description: Vane is cleaning house; lieutenants are turning up dead, and paranoia is spreading through the ranks as alliances shift overnight.

risks: Vane is cleaning house; lieutenants are turning up dead, and paranoia is spreading through the ranks as alliances shift overnight.

actors.agenda: Eliminate anyone who might challenge him.
actors.leverage: He has the most power and the most to lose.

defaultAction.now: Meeting with his inner circle, watching them closely.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Locks himself in the penthouse, ruling by decree.

actors.agenda: Survive the purge by staying invisible.
actors.leverage: She knows where the bodies are buried.

defaultAction.now: Watching from the shadows of the saloon.
defaultAction.ifIgnored2Steps: Attempts a coup if Vane seems weak.

clock.name: The Last Man Standing
clock.doom: Vane rules alone, paranoid and unchecked.
clock.pauseCondition: Players expose Vane’s weakness or eliminate him.

trigger.eventDescription: The body is discovered in the precinct.

consequences.narrative: The fate of the Syndicate is decided.

outgoingLeads.information: Vane is purging his own family to secure power.

discovery.description: A letter from Vane to his brother.

outgoingLeads.information: Clara Vance is allied with the Bookkeeper.
A witness saw Vane kill his lieutenant.

discovery.description: A drunk patron tells a story about seeing Vane.

victory.description: The The Syndicate Purge is resolved successfully

failure.description: The The Syndicate Purge ends in failure
consequences: The situation escalates or becomes worse

themes: Vane is cleaning house; lieutenants are turning up dead, and paranoia is spreading through the ranks as alliances shift overnight.

arc:rise-of-the-syndicate.name: Rise of the Syndicate
arc:rise-of-the-syndicate.description: Corruption as infrastructure: tracing the flow of graft from the dockworkers' wages to the Mayor's inner circle, revealing that loyalty is transactional and the Syndicate's power rests on controlled scarcity and the complicity of every rank.

openingScenes: The rain hasn't stopped since Tuesday. You stand under the peeling awning of Precinct 4, watching a uniformed officer wipe water from his cap while a canvas body bag zips shut in the alley. The officer catches your eye, taps his wallet, and nods toward the station. Inside, the desk sergeant is already filling out the paperwork to make the death look like a drowning. He needs you to sign as the responding detective so he can go home to his wife without asking questions.

A typewriter clacks in a windowless room above a smoky speakeasy. Your editor slides a fresh proof across the scarred desk. The story about the cannery foreman beating a union organizer to death is there, but the final paragraph has been cut with a razor blade. Outside, the mother of the injured boy is pacing the wet sidewalk, clutching a rusted pocketknife. She doesn't know her son's story died on the press floor. She only knows she needs justice tonight, and she's running out of patience.

Your knuckles ache from gripping the steering wheel. The sedan idles in the damp gloom of the Municipal Garage, engine purring. You're waiting for the courier to drop off a leather satchel from a mid-level alderman. When he arrives, he doesn't hand over the envelope. He hands you a single brass key and whispers that the vault he swore was empty actually holds the original land deeds for the entire waterfront. Then the garage lights flicker, and heavy footsteps echo down the concrete ramp, boots splashing in oily puddles.
plotHooks: A city auditor arrives to inspect the port authority accounts. He brings a ledger of his own—one that lists every corrupt official by name and pay grade. The Syndicate wants him gone quietly, but the auditor refuses to leave until he sees the evidence with his own eyes. The party must decide whether to guide him through the truth or feed him a forgery that buys them another month of breathing room.

Three night-shift workers from the Cannery District have been clocked in for seven days straight. Their families swear they haven't seen them since last Thursday. The company claims it's a scheduling error, but the timecards bear identical handwriting, and the men's work coats are hanging in the breakroom, stiff with dried harbor brine and smelling faintly of machine oil.

A beloved street preacher who always spoke plain truth is found slumped over his pulpit, a perfectly clean bullet hole in his chest. His congregation swears he never took a dime from the ward bosses, yet his shoes are polished with imported Italian wax. Someone wanted the one honest voice in the city silenced, but they left no witnesses except the congregation themselves, who are now being pressured to sign affidavits saying he owed money to the wrong people.

A fixer hires the party to retrieve a stolen safe-cracking manual from a rival gang's warehouse. Inside the manual, they find marginalia written in three different hands—each belonging to a different precinct captain. The tool wasn't stolen to crack a bank; it was stolen to crack the police department's chain of command, proving the badges on their chests are just rented props.
commonConflicts: The Weight of the Receipt: Every favor called in carries a price tag. When players use their network or burn a bridge, the city demands payment in loyalty, reputation, or blood. The conflict arises when two debts collide and force a choice between saving a friend or keeping their license to operate, forcing them to weigh conscience against survival.

Bureaucracy as a Weapon: Opponents don't always draw guns; they deploy zoning laws, delayed subpoenas, and shifted jurisdiction lines. Combat becomes a race against red tape, where winning requires forging permits, bribing clerks, or exploiting loopholes faster than the enemy can file injunctions, turning paperwork into a battlefield.
resolutionPatterns: The Pyrrhic Ledger: Truth is uncovered, but exposing it collapses the delicate ecosystem holding the city together. Players secure their objective, yet watch as innocent collateral falls to the machinery they've disturbed. They walk away richer in knowledge but heavier in guilt, knowing the rain will wash the streets clean by morning but the stain remains on their hands.

The Bought Silence: Victory is purchased with compromised principles. Players take the money, burn the evidence, or let the guilty walk free because the alternative would destroy everything they've built. The resolution feels earned but hollow, cementing their place within the very system they once despised, trading honesty for the luxury of staying alive.
